project_constraints.charset_note: Gate FALHOU: a fonte do jogo não tem diacríticos — pangrama pt-BR renderiza como '@' (evidência in-game: artifacts/char1.png, char2.png). Estratégia adotada: TRANSLITERAÇÃO na gravação (acento→ASCII) no reinsert.py; a tradução canônica mantém os acentos. Ver decision_log.md.

glossary_subset.notes: Prato (11_09): wrap de 'pele de amam' recheado de carne/legumes/peixe, molhado em molho. Manter o nome. 'amam skin'->'pele de amam' (Kuon chama de 'pele'; Haku nota que e grao assado, nao couro - manter o trocadilho 'pele').
Variante GRANDE do Gigiri ('o enorme'). NAO e personagem (Boro = qualificador de tamanho). Manter o composto.
Unidade militar do Ukon -> 'Coorte do Ukon'. Traduzir 'Cohort'->'Coorte' consistente.
Criaturas/monstros altamente venenosos (cap.12). Nome de mundo — manter.
Nome dado ao protagonista por Kuon em 11_02 (0x12668). reveal_timing: cena 11_02. Antes disso ele é sem nome.
Rotulo de falante (53 00) da dona da estalagem da vila (descoberto em 11_06, 0x18eb8). Feminino. Voz calorosa, trata por 'querida'. Como Garota/Mulher/Homem: rotulo exibido in-game.
Rótulo de falante (memória); identidade não revelada — genérico.

Erudito/escriba/mago comico e franzino do grupo do Ukon (cap.12); magia de fogo com cantico+danca longos. Fala em registro ARCAICO e comico (pleaseth/mine own; gag de cicio s->sh). Gruda no Haku como 'benfeitor'.
Criatura imortal do subsolo. Nome próprio do mundo — manter.

Guerreiro espirituoso e cativante que faz amizade com Haku ao ve-lo consertar o moinho (cap.12); lidera a 'Coorte do Ukon'. Maroro o trata por 'Mestre Ukon'. SPOILER: identidade verdadeira e reveal de arco posterior — NAO antecipar; no cap.12 e um personagem por si so. reveal_timing: alem do cap.12.

lines: Características: acolhe sem pressionar ("Apenas relaxe..."); curiosa mas paciente; calor humano.
Red flags: soar fria, clínica ou impaciente; formalidade excessiva.
Haku (protagonista, narração 1ª pessoa) — `voice_criticality: high`. Predomina nas 2 cenas:
Características: sentenças curtas e definitivas; duplo sentido ("um mundo totalmente novo te espera"); ambiguidade ameaçadora.
Red flags: soar caloroso/paternal; resolver a ambiguidade (a fala deve permanecer dúbia).
Registro: erudito cômico, pomposo e arcaico; bajulador do Ukon; alívio cômico.
Características: fala arcaica/empolada (equivalente a "vós/peço-vos/meus olhos não me enganam") + gag de cicio (s→sh, ex.: "SHEEKETH"/"shir"); melodramático; gruda no Haku como "benfeitor".
Red flags: perder o arcaísmo ou o cicio (são a piada); soar moderno/neutro; explicar a comédia. Manter o contraste entre a pompa do vocabulário e a fragilidade do personagem.
Características: frases quebradas por reticências; perguntas curtas ("Quem... é você...?"); pouca pontuação forte.
Red flags: falas fluentes/articuladas demais; perder o tom de torpor; pontuação "limpa" que apaga a fragmentação.
Registro: guerreiro carismático, espirituoso, caloroso e informal; líder nato que trata Haku como um irmão mais novo.
Características: desembaraçado, brincalhão, generoso; fala direta e cativante; autoridade leve (lidera a Coorte) sem arrogância.
Red flags: soar rígido/formal/militar demais (a graça é justamente o contraste com a patente); frieza. SPOILER: não deixar a tradução insinuar a identidade verdadeira — no cap.12 ele é só "Ukon".

decisions.title: Opcode de RÓTULO DE FALANTE `53 00` + reconcile de speaker (data-driven)
decisions.summary: **Problema:** in-game, o rótulo de falante aparecia em **inglês** ("Girl") mesmo com a tradução ("Garota") aprovada e gravada. RE: o nome do falante usa um **2º opcode de ponteiro, `53 00`** (mesmo formato file-relativo do `50 00` de diálogo), que o conector ignorava. Resultado: "Girl"→"Garota"

decisions.title: Gate de charset pt-BR — método e veredito
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Marcar `target_charset_supported: likely` e exigir confirmação in-game antes de produção. **Alternativas consideradas:** - Confirmar por presença no texto-fonte — **insuficiente**: o fonte é inglês e quase não usa acentos (só `õ` e `À` aparecem em texto real).

decisions.title: Anomalia 0x33f9 — texto PT/EN corrompido na fonte
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Marcar a linha `0x33f9` como anomalia de fonte (não traduzir em cima do lixo; tratar como linha de sistema reescrita). **Razão:** A string original já vem misturada PT/EN e truncada ("...SISTEMAS AM . RESTARTING...") no próprio jogo. Não é erro de extração. O conector deve sinali

decisions.title: Charset — transliteração na gravação (gate FALHOU)
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Marcar `target_charset_supported: false` e **transliterar** (acento → ASCII) na gravação do binário. A tradução canônica (`approved_translations.csv`, `translation_plan.json`) **mantém os acentos** (correta para QA/revisão); apenas os bytes escritos no jogo são dobrados para ASCI

decisions.title: Escopo cognitivo — 75 → 1025 linhas (cenas 11_01 + 11_02); reveal de Haku in-corpus
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Re-rodar o pipeline completo em escala (cenas 11_01 + 11_02 = 1025 linhas). Novos termos canônicos: **Kuon** (nome revelado em 0x108db), **Haku** (nome dado ao protagonista em 0x12668 — reveal agora **dentro do corpus**), **Tatari** (criatura imortal), **aperyu** (vestimenta), **

decisions.title: CORREÇÃO CRÍTICA — ponteiros são FILE-RELATIVOS, não absolutos
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Ao investigar o "opcode de início de bloco", descobri que **`50 00`+uint32 é um offset RELATIVO ao início do arquivo (Pack)**, não absoluto. Endereço da string = `file_start_do_site + uint32`. Prova: dos ~47k sites, **42.101** só apontam para string como file-relativos vs **63**

tm_voice.speaker: Haku
tm_voice.source: Well, guess the sun still rises no matter where\n
tm_voice.target: Enfim, o sol nasce em qualquer lugar, pelo visto\n

tm_voice.speaker: Haku
tm_voice.source: I am. Still... What am I supposed to do now...?
tm_voice.target: Pois é. Mesmo assim... O que é que eu faço agora...?

tm_voice.speaker: Garota
tm_voice.source: Hey, you there! Could you spare a moment?
tm_voice.target: Ei, você aí! Pode me dar um momento?

tm_voice.speaker: Garota
tm_voice.source: Hey, I'm sorry for bothering you, but could I ask\n

tm_voice.speaker: Maroro
tm_voice.source: Master Ukon! It pleaseth my heart to report my\n
tm_voice.target: Mestre Ukon! É com grande satisfação que reporto que meus\n

tm_voice.speaker: Maroro
tm_voice.source: belongings lay duly unpack'd, and await porters.
tm_voice.target: meus pertences estão desfeitos e aguardam os carregadores.

tm_voice.speaker: Maroro
tm_voice.source: I am VERY tired, sir. Naught more now do I desire\n
tm_voice.target: Estou MUITO cansado, senhor. Nada mais desejo agora\n

tm_voice.speaker: Ukon
tm_voice.source: Really, Maroro? Seems like you get tired quicker\n
tm_voice.target: É sério, Maroro? Parece que você se cansa mais rápido\n

tm_voice.speaker: Homem
tm_voice.source: The way you were carrying on, you got us all\n
tm_voice.target: Do jeito que você estava, nos deixou todos\n

spoiler_guards.fact: A identidade verdadeira de Ukon (ele e mais do que um guerreiro espirituoso).
spoiler_guards.guard: Traduza as falas de/sobre Ukon como o personagem 'Ukon' por si so. NAO insinue outra identidade, patente oculta ou disfarce. Mantenha qualquer ambiguidade do original.

lines.source: Here's to our victory, men! Cheers!
Ukon's Cohort
Cheeeeers!
The inn's dining hall fills with laughter and\n
jovial chatter, giving it a pleasant ambience.
After the Tatari arrived...
Once it digested the body of the Boro-Gigiri, it\n
quietly slunk away back into its cave.
From what I've been told, they're loath to come out\n
of their pits, so we shouldn't have to worry.
And Ukon's company cleaned up the rest of the\n
gigiri, so...
Travelers can use that mountain pass without any\n
danger, now.
Innkeeper
Come now, drink up.
Phew...
It's nice to drink and relax like this.
Upon hearing of our victory, the innkeeper broke\n
out some of her best sake to share with everyone.
I had to water it down to be able to drink it.\n
It's good straight, yeah, but I shouldn't get\n
wasted.
It's got a peculiar flavor, but it's not bad.\n
Just... distinct.
I wash down a bite of amamunii with the sake as\n
I watch the crowd.
Meanwhile, Kuon has been sitting next to me staring\n
into her cup, not saying a word.
What's wrong? Not to your liking?
It's not that it's bad--a little strong, maybe,\n
but...
At a time like this, it'd be nice to have some\n
mead around.
Despite her disappointed tone, Kuon goes on eating\n
and drinking heartily all the same.
Hey, you kids drinkin' up?
Ukon makes his way over with a particularly...\n
intimidatingly sized cup, his face already red.
C'mon, why don't you join us instead of lurking in\n
the corner over here?
You're the stars of this party, after all. Nothing\n
wrong with soaking in the attention a little, huh?
I'll pass. I'm not too eager to sit in the middle\n
of a crowd of sweaty guys.
Same here. I'm... not really a crowd person.
Ahahaha! You really are something, y'know that,\n
kid? Your plan went off without a hitch!
That... really wasn't enough to call a "plan."\n
I was playing things pretty off-the-cuff.
That's just fine.
You came up with it under pressure and executed,\n
and got results. It's impressive, staying cool\n
like that.
Most guys in your situation would've buckled or\n
panicked, not scheme a way out.
You're overpraising me.
I don't know if you're being modest or this is just\n
how you are, but I'm liking you more an' more.
Though, hey, full disclosure--I admit I was pretty\n
worried by how much the hike wore you out.
Guh... Not like I have a leg to stand on with that.
And you shone back there, too, missy.
Me?
Yeah. If you hadn't lured that Tatari out of its\n
lair, it all would've been for nothing.
I didn't think you'd actually pull it off!\n
Color me surprised you did something so\n
reckless.
Honestly, when you came up with that plan, I thought\n
you were joking to lighten the mood.
Was it that reckless?
C'mon, now. If I'd told you to do what she did,\n
would you be able to carry out that order?
No, I'd tell you it's impossible...
Kuon having to pull me along the first time we ran\n
rises in my mind.
I sure as hell couldn't run like that, that's for\n
sure.
Ahaha, I'm just a little nimble, that's all.
I see. We'll leave it at that, I guess.
Anyway! The gigiri extermination was a success\n
thanks to you two joining us.
Please accept my gratitude.
Ukon bows deeply.
C'mon, you're giving us too much credit...
Friendsh, Yamatansh, COUNTRYmen! Exschlude your\n
DEARESHT Maroro nnnot from your rrrevelsh...\n
What SHAY ye?
Ha! And here's our other conquering hero.
A white shadow, its face fast becoming a fearful\n
red, comes wriggling across toward us.
Oh ho HO! Dosht thou partake of mmmosht SHWEET\n
libations? Come, wet thy shpirit with SHMOOTHESHT\n
sake.
Urk. He reeks of booze...
Since he killed the bulk of the gigiri with his\n
magic, Maroro's being hailed as a hero, too.
He's being poured drink after drink, and keeps\n
accepting. Guy's already drunk as a fish.
Accoladesh and bonush pay alike FLING themshelves\n
upon my person thanksh to your aid! Nyoho HO!
Thanks, but I didn't really... do anything that\n
warrants a whole lot of praise.
Ah, ah, pray silence thy proteshts! Th'art SHUCH\n
a hhhumble man, Mashter Haku, truuuuly.
Maroro prods my cheek with his finger. Please,\n
please leave me alone.
ForSHOOTH, thou nnnnurshed me to health through\n
our journey'sh perils, and protected me from the\n
loathshome gigiri.
Thou art my BOSHOM friend, Mashter Haku.
Now I'm his "bosom friend." Great. I've ascended\n
the ranks in his eyes.
And now he's totally attached to me. Cripes.
Hey, missy. Weren't you saying something about\n
heading for the capital?
Mm. We're planning to leave first thing tomorrow.
Meanwhile, Kuon and Ukon have moved on to a\n
completely different subject.
Hey. You two. Help me out, here.
Gonna do more sightseeing while you're there?
There's that, but... it's more to find a job for\n
Haku, I think.
The kid, huh? So he's not your attendant after all?
Wh--Who's an attendant? She's taking care of me\n
because I was an AMNESIAC LOST IN THE MOUNTAINS.
Ha! Wouldn't it be better for appearances' sake,\n
though? And what's this about having amnesia?
I don't even remember my own name. "Haku" is an\n
alias Kuon gave me.
Since I've accepted looking after him, it's my duty\n
to see him become safely independent.
And because he's not really suited to physical labor,\n
I thought we'd try our luck in the capital.
Fortunately, he's quite a wise person.
I see...
Ukon takes a gulp from his cup, downing its contents\n
in one go. He places the empty cup on the table.
Aahh. Well, if that's the case, why don't you come\n
along with us?
To the capital, you mean?
Yeah. We originally came out here to pick up some\n
cargo and escort it back to the capital.
Since the cargo got delayed, I decided to take up\n
the gigiri job, and then...
Well, we know how that turned out.
Ukon's expression grows slightly distant.
I've got no choice but to leave the incapacitated\n
here in the village, so we're down a few men.
And if you join up with us, you'll be paid, 'course.
Kuon would be coming onboard as an apothecary, and\n
the kid... Well, I guess "consultant" works for you.
It's good money. We've got plenty of funds from the\n
reward for the gigiri hunt, after all.
Aren't you worried, kid? Traveling alone with a\n
delic--fragi--vuln--...alone with a young lady?
Why did you keep correcting yourself just now,\n
I wonder?
Didn't mean anything by it, promise. Two people\n
could get outnumbered easily, that's all.
It's safer to travel with more companions. More\n
fun, too. What d'you say?
Mm...
Thanks for the offer, but I think we'll have to\n
respectfully decline.
Aw, come on. How come?
With the number of men you brought with you, that\n
cargo must be something pretty valuable.
Hm. Yeah, I guess you could say that.
With that in mind, the risk of a bandit attack on\n
your company to get to the cargo is too high.
Oh, no, the cargo's guards'll be augmenting our\n
numbers. No bandit would be dumb enough to try it.
But I won't make a pest of myself asking you about\n
it. Just think it over, yeah?
With that, Ukon returns to the throng of the party.
